purpose: Classify Iris authority and cleanup surfaces so future rounds can decide whether files are current, historical, reproduction, or stale before deleting or moving anything.
authority_order: Iris current contract files named in this manifest
historical closeouts, round ledgers, staging artifacts, generated output

current.meaning: Live authority input for Iris contracts, source facts, runtime source, validation commands, or build/package baselines.
current.default_delete_move_policy: protected; requires a focused approved plan and named validation.

historical.meaning: Predecessor, closeout, provenance note, prior plan, or evidence record. It explains current state but is not current authority unless re-adopted by a current entry.
historical.default_delete_move_policy: archive/move only after references are ledgered and no current path depends on it.

reproduction.meaning: Build input, generated output, staging packet, test fixture, package snapshot, baseline, log, or script family needed to recreate or verify a result.

stale.meaning: Superseded, deleted, or not a current authority input. Existence in git history is not restoration authority.
stale.default_delete_move_policy: may remain deleted or be cleaned only after manifest coverage and reference checks; restore requires explicit successor/correction scope.

global_rules: Specific path entries override broader glob entries.
If no entry matches, the path is unclassified and must not be deleted or moved.
docs/Iris/** is stale by default and is not restored as current authority.
Historical closeouts do not become current authority because they mention a deleted path.
Staging and generated artifacts are not current policy authority by existence alone.
Iris/build/description/v2/tools/build/*.py must not be archived or deleted by filename glob.
Runtime Lua, package output, and generated data must not be mutated by docs-only authority cleanup rounds.
Round artifacts and ledgers are successor-round inputs, not replacements for docs/DECISIONS.md or docs/ROADMAP.md.

round1a_reference_reconciliation.coverage: 41 exact live references ledgered after Group A contract restoration

docs_iris_policy.allowed_exception: A future round may restore one concrete file only with new input authority, explicit successor/correction scope, and a reason current authority cannot cover the work.
docs_iris_policy.delete_move_policy: deletion can be accepted as stale cleanup only after live references remain reconciled.
docs_iris_policy.notes: Mentions of docs/Iris paths in current files are historical labels or inline provenance after Round 1-A.

build_contract.delete_move_policy: protected; update only with focused build-contract plan.

runtime_source.delete_move_policy: protected; runtime validation required for behavioral changes.

layer3_deployable_runtime_data.notes: Current ROADMAP readpoint keeps Layer 3 runtime data as chunk manifest plus chunk files. Monolith/chunks dual deployment is not authorized.

package_validation.notes: This command is the package verification anchor when package/release scope is explicitly opened.

package_output.notes: Package output is a generated/package snapshot, not source authority by itself.

validation.required_field_check: jq -e '(.classification_definitions.current != null) and (.docs_iris_policy.classification == "stale") and ([.entries[] | select((.path == null and .path_glob == null) or .classification == null or .authority_role == null or .delete_move_policy == null)] | length == 0)' Iris/_docs/authority/iris_current_authority_manifest.json
